problem.title: Anisphia Wynn Palettia and Good Permutations

sources.notes: Construct a permutation with few bad indices based on gcd relations of adjacent/triple values.
Official editorial builds long perfect arrays from even numbers and odd multiples of 3, with a modular-counting bound.

annotation.summary: Construct a permutation of 1..n with a bounded number of bad indices related to common divisors.
annotation.constraints: The construction must work for all n in linear time and meet a constant bad-index bound.
annotation.core_idea: Group many numbers by guaranteed common divisors: even numbers form pairs around arbitrary fillers, and odd multiples of 3 give another disjoint block. Concatenating these near-perfect blocks covers enough positions that only a constant number of indices can remain bad.

solution_variants.name: divisor-block-construction
solution_variants.summary: Partition values into evens, odd multiples of 3, and remaining fillers, then output repeating filler-divisor-divisor blocks.

tags.evidence: The solution is an explicit permutation construction with a proof of the bad-index bound.
The construction relies on common divisors, especially parity and multiples of 3.
Numbers are grouped by shared divisibility classes to force local gcd properties.
tags.description: Partition numbers by divisibility/common-factor classes to construct or count valid structures.
tags.created_reason: This recurring number-theoretic construction pattern is not captured by the broad number theory tag alone.
